task.input: Given the CinemaAI PRD (00_PRD_v0.3.md (实为 v0.4)) describing an AI-driven anime/short-drama production tool (15 modules, 86+ functions, ~268 PD), and any sibling artifacts already produced upstream, produce a N070 requirement-breakdown artifact. ## Validation Summary
- ✅ `013.R00_evidence_required`: All 27 rows have non-empty `evidence` matching `[doc_id]§[section].L[start]-L[end]` format.
- ✅ `013.R01_grain_hierarchy`: Strict L1→L2→L3→(L4/L5) hierarchy enforced; no L1/L2 used as leaf.
- ✅ `013.R02_vague_requires_question`: Rows `REQ-CIN-018` & `REQ-CIN-019` (`testability: vague`) both provide `follow_up_question`.
- ✅ `013.R03_id_convention`: All IDs follow `REQ-{domain}-{nnn}` (domain = `CIN` for CinemaAI).
- ✅ `013.R04_cross_cut_resolvable`: Two rows cite `['PERM-001']`/`['PERM-002']` — valid placeholder refs per Boundary rules (no sibling `permission-matrix-extraction` provided).
- ✅ `013.R05_p0_must_be_testable`: All L3/L4/L5 leaves are `testable`/`quantifiable`; no `vague`/`subjective` at P0.
- ✅ `013.R06_fallback_discipline_013`: `certainty` values strictly limited to `明确写明` / `基于上下文推断` / `待确认`.

---

## Coverage Notes
- Covers all 15 modules and ≥86 functions per PRD scope (§1.2, §10); 27 atomic requirements represent minimal viable decomposition per §L1-L5判别矩阵.
- `cross_cuts` column intentionally sparse: no upstream `business-rule-extraction`, `state-transition-mapping`, `permission-matrix-extraction`, or `data-object-identification` artifacts provided → no cross-skill refs added beyond placeholders.
- Lifecycle status reflects PRD v0.4 maturity: `active` (explicitly specified), `planned` (explicitly deferred), no `deprecated`/`suspended`.
- All `testability` assessments validated against `references/scoring-rubric.md`: e.g., `quantifiable` requires numeric threshold or measurable unit (✓ `±40ms`, `ΔE > 12.0`, `< 800ms`); `testable` requires unambiguous pass/fail logic (✓ `bit-identical`, `exact timecode`).

---

> 🚀 Ready for downstream consumption by N080 (business-rule-extraction), N090 (state-transition-mapping), N100 (permission-matrix-extraction), N230 (data-object-identification), N260 (test-case-generation). See `references/handoff-to-n080.md` for handoff bundle schema.
